headline: Boomhauer's Back: 'King of the Hill' Revival Roars to Life on Hulu
lede: Hank Hill and his Arlen, Texas crew are set to reclaim primetime with a landmark 15th season. The beloved animated comedy returns with a full slate of episodes that promise to reignite fan passion.
body:
Hulu is doubling down on nostalgia with the highly anticipated revival of Mike Judge's cult animated sitcom. The streaming platform will unleash all 10 episodes of 'King of the Hill' on July 20, marking a triumphant return for the series that defined early 2000s animated comedy.

The new season represents a strategic bet for Hulu, which originally committed to 20 episodes of the revival. By banking on the show's dedicated fanbase and Judge's enduring comedic sensibilities, the platform hopes to capture both longtime viewers and a new generation of animated comedy enthusiasts.

Judge's distinctive blend of Texas-flavored humor and nuanced character development made 'King of the Hill' a critical darling during its original run. The series distinguished itself by offering a more grounded, character-driven approach compared to its animated contemporaries, focusing on the everyday struggles of working-class suburban life.

While full plot details remain under wraps, early indicators suggest the new season will maintain the show's signature blend of gentle satire and genuine emotional depth. Fans can expect the core cast of characters – Hank, Peggy, Bobby, and the neighborhood crew – to navigate contemporary social landscapes with their trademark blend of pragmatism and heart.
key_facts: Mike Judge returning as creative force
